overview: witest28-1 is a minimal single-component web application that serves a static "Hello World" page directly from a lightweight frontend web server. The system consists of one deployable web-app component ‚Äî a React/TypeScript SPA served via Nginx ‚Äî accessible via a root URL path. There are no backend service dependencies; all content is static and self-contained within the single container.
requirements: The application must serve an HTML page at the root path '/' responding to HTTP GET with HTTP 200.
The page must display the text 'Hello World' prominently without requiring scroll or interaction.
The page must have a valid document title (e.g., 'Hello World') visible in the browser tab.
The page must render correctly on modern desktop and mobile browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.
Accessing an undefined route must return HTTP 404 rather than crashing the application.
The page must fully load in under 2 seconds on a standard broadband connection.
Total page weight (HTML, CSS, assets) must not exceed 500 KB.
The application must expose a /health endpoint returning HTTP 200.
The page must meet WCAG 2.1 Level A accessibility requirements, including a minimum 4.5:1 color contrast ratio for the greeting text.
The page must be navigable and readable with a screen reader (semantic HTML, ARIA roles where applicable).
The application must not expose sensitive server information (e.g., software version, internal paths) in HTTP response headers.
The application must serve content over HTTPS in any non-local deployment environment.
The application must not accept or process any user-supplied input on the greeting page.
The application must start up and serve requests without manual intervention after deployment.
The application must not crash or return a 5xx error on a valid root-path request under normal conditions.

components.name: hello-world-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: hello-world-web
  description: Static Hello World web application served via Nginx.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the Hello World HTML page.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successful HTML page response.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns 200 OK when the server is healthy.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Server is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all for undefined routes
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Route not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
## Component: hello-world-web

### Overview
Build a minimal React + TypeScript single-page application that renders a "Hello World" greeting page. The app is built with Vite, producing a small static bundle, and served in production via an Nginx Docker container on port 8080.

---

### Tech Stack
- **Language/Framework:** TypeScript / React 18
- **Build Tool:** Vite
- **Server:** Nginx (Alpine)
- **Port:** 8080 (container)
- **App Path in mono-repo:** `/hello-world-web`

---

### Project Structure
```
/hello-world-web
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Dockerfile
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nginx.conf
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ index.html
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ package.json
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ tsconfig.json
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ vite.config.ts
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ src/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ main.tsx
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ App.tsx
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ App.css
```

---

### Implementation Details

#### `src/App.tsx`
- Render a single `<main>` element with `role="main"`.
- Include an `<h1>` with the text **"Hello World"**.
- Use semantic HTML: `<header>`, `<main>`, `<footer>` structure.
- Apply sufficient color contrast: e.g., `#1a1a1a` text on `#ffffff` background (contrast ratio far exceeds 4.5:1).
- Include `lang="en"` on the root `<html>` element (set in `index.html`).
- Do NOT render any forms, inputs, or user-interactive elements.

#### `index.html`
- Set `<title>Hello World</title>`.
- Set `<html lang="en">`.
- Include a `<meta name="description" content="Hello World application">`.
- Include `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">`.

#### `src/App.css`
- Keep styles minimal (< 5 KB).
- Ensure body background is white (`#ffffff`) and text is near-black (`#1a1a1a`).
- Center the greeting with flexbox.
- `h1` font-size at least `2rem`.
- No external font imports (use system fonts to minimize page weight).
- Font stack: `system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif`.

#### `vite.config.ts`
- Standard Vite config; no special plugins needed beyond `@vitejs/plugin-react`.
- Output directory: `dist`.

#### `package.json` scripts
- `build`: `vite build`
- `preview`: `vite preview`

---

### Dockerfile
```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Stage 2: Serve
FROM nginx:1.25-alpine
RUN rm /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/app.conf
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
```

---

### nginx.conf
```nginx
server {
    listen 8080;
    server_name _;

    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html;

    # Hide Nginx version
    server_tokens off;

    # Security headers
    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
    add_header X-Frame-Options "DENY" always;
    add_header Referrer-Policy "no-referrer" always;

    # Health check endpoint
    location /health {
        access_log off;
        return 200 '{"status":"ok"}';
        add_header Content-Type application/json;
    }

    # Serve SPA ‚Äî all routes fall back to index.html for React Router if added later
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
    }

    # Return 404 for assets not found
    error_page 404 /index.html;

    # Do not expose internal server errors with detail
    error_page 500 502 503 504 /index.html;
}
```

**Note:** Since there is only one route (`/`), the SPA fallback to `index.html` is acceptable. The React app itself should render a user-friendly "Page Not Found" view for unknown paths using React Router's catch-all route, returning a visual 404 message (the Nginx `error_page 404` directive handles unknown static assets).

---

### React Router 404 Handling
- Install `react-router-dom`.
- In `App.tsx`, wrap with `<BrowserRouter>` and define:
  - `<Route path="/" element={<HelloPage />} />`
  - `<Route path="*" element={<NotFoundPage />} />`
- `<NotFoundPage>` renders a simple "404 ‚Äì Page Not Found" message. This gives a proper UI 404 for undefined routes.

---

### Accessibility Requirements
- `<html lang="en">` ‚Äî required for screen readers.
- `<h1>` is the first and only heading on the page.
- All text meets WCAG 2.1 Level A (and AA) contrast: `#1a1a1a` on `#ffffff`.
- No images without alt text; no decorative elements that confuse screen readers.
- Page is fully keyboard-navigable (nothing interactive, so trivially satisfied).

---

### Performance Requirements
- Total built bundle size must be under 500 KB (should be well under 100 KB).
- No external CDN resources; everything is self-contained.
- Vite production build minifies and tree-shakes automatically.

---

### Security Requirements
- `server_tokens off` in Nginx hides the Nginx version.
- No user input fields, forms, or query parameter processing.
- No server-side logic; purely static.
- HTTPS is handled at the ingress/load-balancer layer in non-local environments (the container only needs to serve HTTP internally).

---

### Health Check
- `GET /health` ‚Üí `200 OK` with body `{"status":"ok"}` and `Content-Type: application/json`.
- This is handled directly in Nginx (no backend required).

---

### Edge Cases
- Accessing `/undefined-path` ‚Üí React renders `<NotFoundPage>` (visual 404).
- Nginx handles all asset 404s with `error_page 404 /index.html`.
- The app must not crash on any browser (test with Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).
- Mobile viewport must be correctly set via the `<meta viewport>` tag.
